metadata_details.purpose: Parser for generic logs migrated from samples-main
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of generic logs in appropriate format
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes LEEF (Log Event Extended Format) logs with timestamp-based entries, using pattern matching for key-value pairs. It's designed for generic security event processing with vendor-agnostic implementation, focusing on sequential parsing of log entries with timestamp validation and key-value extraction.

factors: Multiple regex patterns for different field types

optimization_opportunities: Implement caching for frequently matched patterns
Add error handling for malformed entries
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: leef_template_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Generic Event (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:07:13.352823

-- Pre-compile patterns for better performance
local timestamp_pattern = "^(%d+)-(%d+)-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)%w+"
local key_pattern = "[%w]+"
local last_value_pattern = "[^=]+"

-- Cached string operations
local format = string.format
local match = string.match
local gsub = string.gsub

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with local variables
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "Generic Event", 
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "Generic",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            version = "1.0.0",
            product = {
                vendor_name = "vendor",
                name = "vendor"
            }
        }
    }

    -- Timestamp transformation with validation
    if record.timestamp then
        local y,m,d,h,min,s = match(record.timestamp, timestamp_pattern)
        if y and m and d and h and min and s then
            -- Convert to UNIX timestamp (milliseconds)
            output.time = os.time({
                year = tonumber(y),
                month = tonumber(m),
                day = tonumber(d),
                hour = tonumber(h),
                min = tonumber(min),
                sec = tonumber(s)
            }) * 1000
        end
    end

    -- Hostname mapping with sanitization
    if record.host then
        output.hostname = gsub(record.host, "[^%w%.%-]", "_")
    end

    -- Dynamic metadata field mapping
    if record.keyPattern then
        local metadata = {}
        for k,v in pairs(record.keyPattern) do
            if type(k) == "string" and type(v) == "string" then
                -- Sanitize keys and values
                local clean_key = gsub(k, "[^%w_]", "_")
                metadata[clean_key] = v
            end
        end
        output.metadata.custom_fields = metadata
    end

    -- Validation of required fields
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os.time() * 1000
    end

    if not output.hostname then
        output.hostname = "unknown_host"
    end

    -- Error checking before return
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    return output
end

-- Cache common string patterns
local function init_cache()
    local cache = {
        patterns = {
            timestamp = timestamp_pattern,
            key = key_pattern,
            value = last_value_pattern
        }
    }
    return cache
end

-- Initialize pattern cache
local CACHE = init_cache()

-- Error handling wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end
